name: Distill documents with Airtable and Slack for your team

categories.description: Documents that are urgent for the contracts team.
Documents that are routine for the contracts team.
Documents that are fyi for the contracts team.

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03277-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Classify with AI

### Classify with AI

LLM classification into: Urgent, Routine, FYI (+fallback). Output port order matches this list.

Ports: ①→ Store as Urgent · ②→ Store as Routine · ③→ Store as FYI · ④→ Alert Slack — Other

### OpenAI Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `gpt-4o-mini`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Classify with AI** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Store as Urgent

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Documents' table.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Store as Routine

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Documents' table.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Store as FYI

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Documents' table.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Alert Slack — Other

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Reunite Branches

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

# Distill documents with Airtable and Slack for your team

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill documents with Airtable and Slack for your team. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual contracts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Classify with AI → Store as Urgent → Reunite Branches

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating contracts in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; OpenAI API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

One Text Classifier node replaces what would otherwise be a chain of brittle IF/Switch keyword checks — the LLM handles the ambiguous cases a rules engine can't. Each category gets its own action node, so a failure in one branch (say, a bad Slack call) never blocks the others; Merge reunites them so the run still reports one clean completion.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a 4th category with its own action branch.
2. Replace one action (e.g. a Slack ping) with a task-creation node and see how the wiring changes.
3. Feed the classifier deliberately ambiguous text and check which fallback category it lands in.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your contracts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
